title: file permissions problem when bind mount a ntfs volume
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

No, this is a HOWTO or question

**Description**
I want to use rootless `mysql` image, but I don't have much space left in my home dir. I have a data disk with ntfs filesystem, so I plan to store mysql data files in that disk and bind mount them to mysql container.

This data disk is mounted by `Thunar` (a file manager for xfce) at location `/run/media/sify/Elements SE`.  To mount I just clicked it under the `Devices` section of `Thunar`. 

By default ntfs-3g doesn't enable file permissions (everything is in mode `777` and owned by me ), which causes problems for files like `my.cnf` in containers (mysql would ignore them if they're world-writable) .

Later I learned that I can create a usermapping file to enable file permissions on ntfs, so I did it with `ntfsusermap` tool. The content of `.NTFS-3G/UserMapping`:
```
# Generated by ntfsusermap for Linux, v 1.2.0
:sify:S-1-5-21-756273107-42706682-1455446346-513
sify:sify:S-1-5-21-756273107-42706682-1455446346-1001
```
I re-mounted the disk, now it has file permissions.

I created a `./data` directory for mysql data files, and a `./conf` directory for mysql configurations, then I try to start mysql container with `podman run -d --rm --name mysql -v ./data:/var/lib/mysql -v ./conf:/etc/mysql/conf.d  -p 3306:3306 --privileged mysql`.

No error output, and a container id is printed out, I thought everything works fine, until I realiazed I can't find my container anywhere (`podman container ls -a` shows nothing).

If I put `data`  and `conf` directory in my home dir (which is ext4 format), everything works fine. I tested a little, and found that `chown` and creating files in `data` dir is not allowed when bind mount to the ntfs disk (they are allowed when bind mount to my home dir)  
```
[sify@fedora Elements SE]$ podman run -it --rm -v ./data:/var/lib/mysql -v ./conf:/etc/mysql/conf.d --privileged mysql bash
root@99ee485902e8:/# cd /var/lib/mysql
root@99ee485902e8:/var/lib/mysql# ls -l
total 0
root@99ee485902e8:/var/lib/mysql# touch a
root@99ee485902e8:/var/lib/mysql# ls -l
total 0
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root 0 Dec  4 10:29 a
root@99ee485902e8:/var/lib/mysql# chown mysql:mysql a
chown: changing ownership of 'a': Operation not permitted
root@99ee485902e8:/var/lib/mysql# su -s /bin/bash mysql
mysql@99ee485902e8:/var/lib/mysql$ touch b
touch: cannot touch 'b': Permission denied
``` 
Why is that? What should I do to bind mount a ntfs volume?

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.2
API Version:  3.4.2
Go Version:   go1.16.8
Built:        Sat Nov 13 19:16:48 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

<Details>


comments.body: First is this an SELinux issue.  Have you tried this with SELinux disabled?
@rhatdan I've tried to change to permissive mode with `setenforce 0`, also tried to disable selinux with `grubby --update-kernel ALL --args selinux=0`, the issue still exists
Kernel 5.15 has a native ntfs driver. Can you try to use this?

@Luap99  Although I'm on fedora 34 with kernel 5.15.6, I can't mount using the new ntfs3 driver:
```
[root@fedora sify]# mount -t ntfs3 /dev/sda1 /run/media/sify/Elements\ SE/
mount: /run/media/sify/Elements SE: wrong fs type, bad option, bad superblock on /dev/sda1, missing codepage or helper program, or other error
```
After searching on the internet for solutions, I read some [bad comments](https://www.reddit.com/r/Fedora/comments/r3cjim/kernel_updated_to_515_should_i_keep_ntfs3g/hmc32es/) saying that ntfs3 driver is not stable now, and it's buggy, slower than ntfs-3g,  and may cause data loss, which holds me back from trying.
Run `podman unshare` and try to create/read/chown files within it. If that does not work the ntfs-3g fuse driver does not support user namespaces.

Somehow the ntfs3 driver is working now, and all operations mentioned above succeed. Things I did (I don't know which step fixed the mounting error):
- upgrade to fedora 35
- repair the ntfs disk using chkdsk (which took a long time, and some data were lost, especially those files I `chmod` when mounting through ntfs-3g)


> Run `podman unshare` and try to create/read/chown files within it. If that does not work the ntfs-3g fuse driver does not support user namespaces.

I tried that, it does not work

Well I am not sure whether or not from the previous reply if the problem has gone away or not. But I know there is nothing Podman can do to fix.  So closing.  We can continue the conversation in this issue though.

I have a similar issue when trying to bind mount a folder from a NTFS partition. The issue happens when I use the SeLinux Z option in docker-compose.yaml. Here's the error that shows up and stops the containers from starting when I run  "docker-compose up": 
```

[myname@fedora myproject]$ docker-compose up
Recreating myproject_db_1 ... error

ERROR: for myproject_db_1  error preparing container 2125e27848ea682dd33fd2b87bd144268ebb3935d102766eba1739fd32035cd7 for attach: lsetxattr /run/media/myname/Ba1/src/myproject/data/cache: operation not supported

ERROR: for db  error preparing container 2125e27848ea682dd33fd2b87bd144268ebb3935d102766eba1739fd32035cd7 for attach: lsetxattr /run/media/myname/Ba1/src/myproject/data/cache: operation not supported
ERROR: Encountered errors while bringing up the project.
```

Does this mean that SeLinux or Podman do not support NTFS?

By the way, not using the Z option does not cause a problem. Seems like NTFS does not need (or support) the Z option.
Yes the :Z is only needed for file systems that support XAttrs.  NTFS is probably labeled with a label that the SELinux container type container_t is allowed to read/write.
